methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $34M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Terrahaptix is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Nathan Nwachuku and Maxwell Maduka that has raised $34M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M), supplemented by a broader comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public defense-tech firms.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $34M raised as a base proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $68M. Peer-median analysis identifies a $300M median valuation across the three same-stage comparables, with a range of $150M to $500M, reflecting material variance in scale and maturity within the cohort.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $58M (low) to $345M (high), with a median point of $184M. The wide spread reflects legitimate uncertainty: stage-progression anchors conservatively to deployed capital, while peer medians capture market pricing for functionally comparable businesses. Key sensitivities include Terrahaptix's product maturity relative to Stark Defence and Vatn Systems, customer concentration and contract structure in the defense vertical, and technical defensibility of its core offering. Resolution of these factors will be critical to narrowing the range in subsequent financing rounds.
